title: No emotion-specific image-computable code in the human amygdala — analysis code
description: Analysis code and result logs for a representation-invariance ("false-floor") test of the claim that the human amygdala carries an emotion-specific, image-computable code. Emotion-supervised encoders are compared against matched generic object/vision encoders across four modalities (naturalistic movie fMRI, face-morph fMRI, single-neuron firing rates, and audio), each with a sensory-cortex positive control. The repository contains only analysis code, the derived result logs that constitute the evidence, and the publication figures; all input data are third-party and public and are not redistributed here. Accompanies the manuscript of the same title (submitted to eLife).
